title: Staurochilus agusanensis
taxonomy: Staurochilus agusanensis is a species of orchid. It belongs to the family Orchidaceae and the genus Staurochilus. Staurochilus agusanensis is native to the Philippines and is found in the wild. The species classification for Staurochilus agusanensis is a type of epiphytic orchid that grows on trees and rocks. It is considered an endangered species due to habitat loss and overcollection.

intro: Staurochilus agusanensis, commonly known as the Agusan staurochilus, is a species of orchid native to the Philippines. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, particularly for its purported health benefits in reducing inflammation, improving respiratory function, and treating fever, due to its anti-inflammatory and antipyretic properties. The bioactive constituents of Staurochilus agusanensis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, Staurochilus agusanensis is used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are commonly consumed to alleviate various health conditions.
